QUESTIONS THAT ACTUALLY MATTERFar beyond “better-looking images,” what truly defines the evolution of an image model is the level of control it provides.1 – GEOMETRY & SPACE• Is there an internal coordinate system (x, y, z)?• Does the model understand real-world units (cm, m, px)?• Can object positions be locked within the scene?• Do objects remain anchored across multiple images?• Can perspective and aspect ratio be fixed per project?2 – ELEMENT-LEVEL CONTROL• Can you use independent seeds per character/object?• Is there LOCAL fine-tuning (not just global)?• Can elements be moved/resized without recreating the entire scene?• Can you modify a single specific detail (e.g., change only the color of a sock)?3 – CONSISTENCY & CONTINUITY• Is there scene version control?• Does the model maintain context/history across generations?• Do zoom and cropping preserve scale and proportion?4 – COLOR & VISUAL IDENTITY• Is there real support for color systems (Pantone, HEX, CMYK, RGB)?• Can you define a global “color bible” per project?• Does the palette remain consistent across images?• Is there fine color control post-generation?5 – PRODUCTION PIPELINE• Is there a concept of a project (not just isolated images)?• Does the workflow enable real consistency for series?The evolution is not just about visual quality.It’s about control.The more control you have, the closer you get to a real professional pipeline.
